_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Right to Repair (Directive (EU) 2024/1799 — DE transposition):**
No producer/EPR-style registration scheme is created by this Directive. The obligations are structural (manufacturer repair duties, seller information duties, warranty extension) rather than registry-based. However, the European Commission is required to establish a European Repair Platform by 31 July 2027, with a common online interface. Registration on this platform by repairers and logistics-adjacent businesses is **voluntary** for commercial operators; it will be free of charge for consumers. Member States must communicate their national contact point for the platform to the Commission by 31 July 2026. There is no mandatory workspace registration obligation at this stage. The workspace should collect and maintain the following operational data fields for covered product categories moving through its network: product category (Annex II classification), manufacturer identity, model/serial number, date of purchase (to establish warranty period), and return/repair routing instructions — these are the data elements that downstream sellers and manufacturers will require to fulfil the new repair and information obligations.

**Medical Registry Law (Drucksache 21/5922):**
The bill establishes a centralised Medical Registry Coordination Centre (Zentrum für Medizinregister) and a Medical Registry Directory (Medizinregisterverzeichnis). Registration in the directory is linked to a voluntary qualification process tied to data-protection and data-security audits. Registries that complete qualification gain access to facilitated data-sharing rules — including the ability to share anonymised or pseudonymised data with third parties for defined purposes (research, quality assurance). The insurance-number-derived pseudonym will serve as a cross-registry linkage key. No freight-forwarder registration obligation is created. Data fields relevant to workspace operations: where the workspace handles implant logistics under the existing Implantateregistergesetz (amended by this bill), it should confirm with clients whether shipping records are required to carry registry-compatible identifiers. The bill's Bundesrat review took place on 8 May 2026 (1065th Bundesrat session).

*Source: Bundestag Drucksache 21/5922, 13.05.2026. https://dserver.bundestag.de/btd/21/059/2105922.pdf; EUR-Lex summary, Directive (EU) 2024/1799. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/EN/legal-content/summary/common-rules-promoting-the-repair-of-goods-and-amending-related-eu-legislation.html*
